rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by fixing unresolved references or removing invalid clauses
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex
Align helper declaration types with expression types
Remove clauses with type <error> instead of adding architecture
generic_rules: Trace the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool
Comparison expressions should be of type bool
Integer literals and arithmetic should be of type int
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex
Align helper declaration types with expression types
Remove clauses with type <error> instead of adding architecture
anti_patterns: Do not add architecture to fix unresolved references
Do not use package-qualified identifiers, data types, or property references
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports should be placed in valid component type scopes
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
